title: hypericum perforatum subsp. perforatum
intro: Hypericum perforatum subsp. perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant in the Hypericaceae family, native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and hyperforin. The health benefits of St. John's Wort include relief from mild to moderate depression, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of ADHD and fibromyalgia. St. John's Wort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
